title: Sync source payload surfaces for doctor closure

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Mirror the doctor/payload surface fixes into source payload locations so future upgrades do not reintroduce drift.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to source payload copies corresponding to the installed surfaces already fixed for doctor closure.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Patch package bootstrap/source payload mirrors and run planning/workspace proof selected for payload authority paths.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A fix requires runtime behavior changes or unrelated package implementation changes.
canonical_core.next_action: Run expanded workspace and planning proof for the source payload alignment.
proof_expectations: make test-workspace and make lint-workspace remain green.
make test-planning and make lint-planning cover the planning bootstrap payload mirror.
doctor remains healthy/compatible with no warnings or review findings.
completion_criteria: Source payload mirrors match the installed surface fixes.

goal: Mirror installed doctor-closure surface fixes into source payload so the next payload update stays current.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: No required continuation remains for this archived slice.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: yes; planning closeout recorded explicit proof input.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: None for this archived slice; reopen only if new evidence invalidates the proof.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: No required continuation remains for this archived slice.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Update the payload to satisfy doctor. This repo must always have up-to-date surfaces
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Installed surfaces and source payload mirrors must both be current and doctor-clean.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Mirror the installed ownership, intent skill, and planning docs fixes into their source payload copies.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Source payload mirrors under src/agentic_workspace/_payload and packages/planning/bootstrap plus closeout planning records.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; make test-planning; make lint-planning; make maintainer-surfaces; doctor; git diff --check.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing package runtime behavior.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated package source or generated artifacts not required by payload-source parity.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Source payload mirrors were patched; run expanded proof, close out, commit, push.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Mirror installed doctor-closure surface fixes into source payload.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep source payload changes aligned to installed payload/doctor fixes only.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Which proof commands to rerun from the router-selected workspace and planning lane.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The source mirror requires runtime implementation changes.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

claim_authorization.renderer_rule: Renderers must choose only templates whose required claim class and closure action are authorized here; unsafe prose examples are diagnostics, not the enforcement model.
